accept: CLAUDE.md sub-agents section explicitly says: spawning a sub-agent in this repo means using isolation:worktree by default; the only safe exception is when no other agent will touch the tree until it finishes.
Rationale captured in versioning-rationale section: explains the git-index serialization gotcha and why op-log concurrency does not save you here.
If a parallel-on-main pattern is ever desired, it is documented separately and is not the default.
payload.description: Discovered 2026-05-10 while running 4 parallel sub-agents. Two un-isolated sub-agents working in the same /Users/andrewcove/Workspace/act tree collide on git index even when their files don't overlap: 'act update --claim' fails with 'error: Please commit or stash them' if any other agent has uncommitted writes anywhere in the tree. The op-log model handles file-level concurrency (different op-files never conflict textually) but the auto-commit step serializes on git index for the working directory. Two worktree-isolated agents (act-acd9, act-63a1) ran fine in parallel; the un-isolated one (act-5467) blocked me from claiming a different issue in the same tree until it finished. CLAUDE.md's sub-agents section currently says 'whether to spawn sub-agents is a harness decision'. It should also say: when you do spawn them for this repo, isolation:worktree is mandatory unless you can guarantee they finish before any other writer touches the tree.
payload.title: CLAUDE.md must require isolation:worktree when spawning sub-agents
